text: The surgeon injects saline mixed with methylene blue beneath the lesion to elevate it, ensuring a clear margin for resection.

The surgeon places the snare two to three millimeters below the pedunculated polyp and uses the Endocut Q-three-one-three setting to perform a clean resection. After the resection, the base is examined for bleeding and perforation, with neither observed.
The surgeon observes a tattoo marking at the base of the resected area and applies three hemostatic clips sequentially from the top end downward to close the defect and prevent delayed bleeding.
